{"key_event":"Cedar Revolution amid Hezbollah-state dual power","reasoning":"Civil society power rose as mass mobilization, independent media/NGOs, sectarian parties, and Hezbollah’s autonomous networks showed major capacity to organize and constrain the state, though polarization limited national accountability. State power slipped because Syrian withdrawal improved formal sovereignty but the state still lacked a monopoly on violence, faced fiscal strain, war damage, institutional paralysis, and security gray zones despite some gains for the army in 2007.","society_change":1.0,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":-0.5,"state_power":4.7}
